items.title: Let’s Get Together: Toward an Integration of Personality Psychology and Distinct Emotions Research
items.author: Eric J. Mercadante, Aaron C. Weidman, Jessica L. Tracy
items.content: <article>Perspectives on Psychological Science, Ahead of Print. Emotions play a prominent role in personality psychology, yet personality researchers most frequently study them as broad dimensions (e.g., negative affect) rather than distinct emotions (e.g., fear). We argue that a greater incorporation of distinct ...</article>

items.content: <article>Perspectives on Psychological Science, Ahead of Print. Cognitive dysfunction is essential to conceptualizing, defining, and assessing much of psychopathology. items.content: <article>Perspectives on Psychological Science, Ahead of Print. Meta-analyses have consistently demonstrated the robustness of the cross-race effect (CRE; i.e., better recognition of same-race faces compared with different-race faces).
